mission.title: linkerd: Create chocolatey package for Windows CLI releases
mission.description: Create chocolatey package for Windows CLI releases. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
## Feature Request

### What problem are you trying to solve?
Simplify installation of CLI on Windows machines
### How should the problem be solved?
Create Chocolatey package to allows users to install CLI using `choco install ...`

What do you want

steps.title: Apply the fix for Create chocolatey package for Windows CLI releases
steps.description:
This draft PR aims to fix #3063 by building a chocolatey package for linkerd2's Windows CLI
The package currently exists in the folder `windowspkg`

To build (on windows system):
- Install chocolatey
- cd into `windowspkg/linkerd2-cli`
- Run `choco pack`
- Run `choco install linkerd2-cli -s . ` , make sure cmd is running as administrator
- Run `refreshenv` to add linkerd2-cli path to system
- To unistall run `choco uninstall linkerd2-cli`

The following is further work to be done:
- Fill

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Create chocolatey package for Windows CLI releases" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
